statute: Arizona Revised Statutes § 32-1101 et seq. (Registrar of Contractors); A.R.S. § 33-981 (Mechanics Lien Law)
license_label: Arizona Registrar of Contractors (ROC) License Number
license_notes: All contractors must be licensed with the Arizona Registrar of Contractors (ROC). Both residential and commercial work requires ROC licensing.

mandatory_clauses.label: ROC license disclosure
Workmanship standard
mandatory_clauses.text:
WORKMANSHIP STANDARD

All work shall be performed in a workmanlike manner and in accordance with applicable building codes and the Arizona Registrar of Contractors standards. Any defective workmanship shall be corrected at no additional cost to the homeowner.

mandatory_clauses.label: Preliminary notice / mechanics lien disclosure
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS (PRELIMINARY TWENTY-DAY NOTICE)

Under Arizona law (A.R.S. § 33-992.01), persons who furnish labor, professional services, materials, machinery, fixtures, or tools to your project may have a right to file a mechanics lien against your property if they are not paid, provided they have given you a preliminary 20-day notice. Even if you pay your general contractor, unpaid subcontractors and suppliers who provided a preliminary 20-day notice may still have lien rights.

To protect yourself:
1. Request a list of all subcontractors and suppliers from your contractor
2. Obtain lien waivers from all parties before making final payment
3. Make checks jointly payable to the contractor and any known lien claimants

mandatory_clauses.label: ROC complaint rights notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
CONTRACTOR COMPLAINT NOTICE

If you have a complaint about the work performed under this contract, you may file a complaint with the Arizona Registrar of Contractors at:

Arizona Registrar of Contractors
1700 W. Washington Street, Suite 105
Phoenix, AZ 85007
Phone: (602) 542-1525
Website: www.azroc.gov

Complaints must generally be filed within two years of the project completion date.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)

penalties_for_non_compliance: Contracting without ROC license = criminal offense. ROC can revoke license. Consumer may file complaint with ROC, which can mandate corrective work.
notes: Arizona requires ROC licensing for all contractor work. Written contracts required for jobs over $1,000. The preliminary 20-day lien notice system is critical — subcontractors and suppliers must provide this notice within 20 days of first furnishing services/materials to preserve lien rights. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation requirements. Arizona's ROC has active enforcement and a Consumer Recovery Fund for homeowner claims.
